ORDER DENYING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-10-2022 HEARING RE: PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION IS DENIED. MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-18-2023 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-18-2023 HEARING RE: PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER'S MOTION FOR COSTS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR $1,378.66 IN COSTS IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Motion And Notice Of Hearing For Costs
Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER's Motion For Costs.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner's unopposed motion for $1,378.66 in costs is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-10-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Petitioner ANTHONY SINGER (Tel: 415-278-1843) in pro per;
Atty. TARA M. STEELEY, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-4655) for respondent City and County of San Francisco sued herin as San Francisco Police Department.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER's Petition for Writ Of Mandate.

Matter argued and submited. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Anthony Singer's "petition for a writ of mandate compelling production of public records" is denied.

Filed in May 2022, Singer's petition seeks from the San Francisco Police Department (1) "specific records requested" under the California Public Records Act (CPRA) and (2) "information for all arrestees on a rolling basis" into the future. (Pet. 1:18-19, 3:1-4.)

First, the police declare under penalty of perjury that the "specific records requested" have been produced to Singer. (Rueca Dec. 2:14-18; see also Parker v. Bowron (1953) 140 Cal.2d 344, 351 [no writ where no practical benefit to petitioner]).

Second, Singer's blanket demand for "information for all arrestees" into the future finds no support in the law; rather, CPRA requests for specific records must be filed. (Rosenthal v. Hansen (1973) 34 Cal.App.3d 754.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR No. 13714, Email: apourtabib@gmail.com, Tel: 415-601-7054 (302/RBU)
